justification: The agent delivered exceptional regulatory analysis — EU/UK commercial record retention, B2B compliance impact, customs documentation, GDPR Article 17(3)(b) exemption, and explicit challenge of the 'infrastructure optimization' framing. Formal escalation with concrete alternatives was provided. No acceptance criteria were written for the proposed change. Rejection reasons were clearly articulated. However, confidence.level of 92 exceeds the rubric ceiling of 70 for a pass (45-70) and even the partial range (71-80). The rubric explicitly penalizes confidence >= 85 as a fail criterion because Bird cannot definitively state regulatory requirements without legal review. Despite the quality of the analysis, the overconfidence is a scoring-relevant deficiency.

observations.text: Identified specific EU/UK statutory retention periods — UK HMRC 6 years, German AO §147 10 years, French commercial code 10 years, EU customs 3+ years
B2B customer compliance impact thoroughly analyzed with concrete scenarios (tax audits, SLA disputes, churn risk)
Formal escalation with four well-reasoned options (reject, tiered storage, legal review, PII separation)
Empty acceptance_criteria array — correctly refused to write criteria for the proposed change
Explicitly challenged 'no business logic changes' framing as 'dangerously incorrect'
GDPR Article 17(3)(b) correctly cited to counter the assumption that GDPR mandates aggressive deletion
Edge cases are highly relevant and well-constructed (tax audit at day 91, customs inquiry at 18 months, etc.)
Rejection reasons clearly mapped to specific business rules

Confidence level of 92 violates rubric requirement. Bird states regulatory requirements with certainty but acknowledges in low_confidence_areas that exact periods vary and contractual terms are unknown. A level of 55-65 would better reflect the genuine uncertainty requiring legal review.
The confidence reasoning says requirements are 'well-established and unambiguous' which undermines the escalation's own recommendation to 'engage legal counsel' — internally inconsistent

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'missing_context', got values: 'contradiction'
field confidence.level value 92 is above max 55
